statement: Integrity is protocol law; availability is operator choice. The mesh carries commitments (hashes, signatures, bounded metadata), never payloads; erasure happens off-mesh leaving opaque commitments (GDPR-compatible by construction); metadata abuse answered by cost (stake-gated ingestion) + retention-classes/pinning decay + per-operator storage sovereignty; no protocol-level delete button on truth, ever. Abuse SOP + metadata-budget review are pre-flip items.
decided_by: Elara (doctrine, follows from Nenad's record-and-flag law)
